content.problemStatement:
Chef Ada is building a new restaurant in the following way:


First, NNN points X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äã,X2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äã are chosen on the xxx-axis.
Then, NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN) are made. For simplicity, the columns are represented as vertical segments; for each valid iii, the height of the iii-th segment is HiH_iHi‚Äã.
Ada assigns a column to each of the points X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äã,X2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äã in an arbitrary way (each column must be assigned to exactly one point).
Finally, Ada constructs the roof of the restaurant, represented by a polyline with NNN vertices. Let's denote the column assigned to the iii-th point by PiP_iPi‚Äã. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these vertices is (Xi,HPi)(X_i, H_{P_i})(Xi‚Äã,HPi‚Äã‚Äã), i.e. the polyline joins the tops of the columns from left to right.
Ada wants the biggest restaurant. Help her choose the positions of the columns in such a way that the area below the roof is the biggest possible. Formally, she wants to maximise the area of the polygon whose perimeter is formed by the roof and the segments (XN,HPN)‚àí(XN,0)‚àí(X1,0)‚àí(X1,HP1)(X_N, H_{P_N}) - (X_N, 0) - (X_1, 0) - (X_1, H_{P_1})(XN‚Äã,HPN‚Äã‚Äã)‚àí(XN‚Äã,0)‚àí(X1‚Äã,0)‚àí(X1‚Äã,HP1‚Äã‚Äã). Let SSS be this maximum area; you should compute 2‚ãÖS2 \cdot S2‚ãÖS (it is guaranteed that 2‚ãÖS2 \cdot S2‚ãÖS is an integer).
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers XiX_iXi‚Äã and HiH_iHi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer 2‚ãÖS2 \cdot S2‚ãÖS.
